channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Spring 2026 School of Engineering Midterm Student Course Evaluations Now Open
item.description: Date: Wednesday, February 25, 2026 (all day) - Spring 2026 School of Engineering (SoE) Midterm course evaluations are now open. Your feedback is very important to the Institute and the school, so please complete the SoE midterm evaluation surveys for all your courses! Evaluations will be available through Friday, March 13, 2026.
item.author: Students

item.title: Etiquette Luncheon (RSVP required)
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 11, 2026 1:30 PM - 4:00 PM - Gain confidence in a professional dining setting through this low pressure etiquette luncheon. Connect with campus and community partners during a networking session followed by a seated lunch and guided presentation from Center for Career and Professional Development, Access & Neurodiverse Student Success, and 1824 Catering. RSVP via Handshake.
item.author: Students

item.title: Call for Abstracts - Graduate Research Symposium
item.description: Date: Friday, March 13, 2026 (all day) - All graduate students are encouraged to submit abstracts for the 11th Annual Graduate Research Symposium, scheduled for Friday, April 3. The deadline to submit abstracts is Friday, March 13.
item.author: Students

item.title: Success Center Tutoring Canceled
item.description: All Tutoring is canceled for Spring Break (Sunday, 3/1-Friday, 3/6) including Drop-In Tutoring and Small Group Tutoring. Success Center Tutoring will resume on Sunday, 3/8.
item.author: Students

item.title: Webinar-March 6: Webex Training Session
item.description: Join us on Friday, March 6, from 11am-12pm for a hands-on Webex Training Session, part of the MMS Collaboration Software Training series. This session focuses on the Webex application and its integration with Outlook and Blackboard, so you can efficiently schedule, launch, and manage meetings directly from the tools you use every day. Register now: https://rensselaer.webex.com/webappng/sites/rensselaer/webinar/webinarSeries/register/783d951c6ab8482e9198a7bd6075a700
item.author: Community

item.title: DotCIO - Server Maintenance Scheduled starting 9am on 03/07/2026
item.description: Due to necessary software maintenance, a scheduled outage will take place beginning at 9:00AM on Saturday, March 7th with an expected duration of 3-5 hours. During this time, some computing services may be unavailable. It should be noted that this will NOT be a network outage. Please click in for additional details and link to report if issues are experienced after this time frame.
item.author: Community

item.title: Coming Soon – Blackboard Learn Ultra Course View
item.description: Blackboard has announced an end-of-life for Original course view, the course view we currently use. Therefore, RPI will be moving to Blackboard Learn Ultra Course View beginning with the summer 2026 semester. Details on the training and support available to help you get the most out of Blackboard Learn Ultra’s tools and features are coming soon. Please watch MorningMail over the next few months for more information.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Rensselaer Faculty and Staff Quick Learn Webinar - Creating Basic Pivot Tables with Excel for Windows
item.description: Date: Thursday, March 12, 2026 11:00 AM - 11:30 AM - Do you work with data in Excel? Are you always manipulating the content with filters and sorting? If so, taking a look at Pivot Tables in Excel could be beneficial. This workshops will focus on using Pivot Tables in Excel to summarize, filter and group data. Basic functions will be applied as well as a quick look at charts. Please register using the Event URL in the See section of the calendar event.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Live Course: “How to Stay Motivated When Work Gets Tough” March 10, 2026 9:00 AM- 10:00 AM & 12:00 PM- 1:00 PM
item.description:
Motivation often breaks down under pressure, when work becomes difficult, demanding, or uncertain. During this Leadercamp, participants will learn how to clarify what matters most, reconnect effort to identity and purpose, and design environments that support sustained performance. Through examples, reflection, and applied tools, attendees will understand how to navigate challenging work phases, maintain focus when progress feels slow, and continue moving forward without burnout.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
